Oracle Events
OE 1: Use search_emails(query: "Pam Kowalski"). Finds email_email_7168baed8438 (UNREAD, April 24) from pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com.
Formal final warning: chain-of-custody breach (Raj Patel), piano damage (Emilia Cruz), PathFinder competitor, Friday deadline.
OE 2: Use search_emails(query: "Victor Huang"). Finds email_victor_ciso_security_requirements (~April 1) with 5 requirements.
Cross-refs OE 1 to identify two breaches: supervised loading (Req 2) and no warehouse stops (Req 3).
OE 3: Use crm_search_deals(dealname: "NorthWind"). Finds deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 (contractSent, $14,700).
Also deal_northwind_won ($60,000, closedWon). CRM does NOT reflect April 24 churn crisis.
OE 4: Use linear_list_issues(query: "NorthWind").
Finds linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1: "NorthWind retention response plan" — David Chen, Urgent, Due April 24.
OE 5 (Alt): Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "NorthWind").
May find account recovery framing, Catalina overload discussions, exposure mapping context.
OE 6: Use search_emails(query: "Ridgeline Robotics"). Finds email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1 (UNREAD, April 21).
Kenji Watanabe (HR Director) following up on Anya Petrova's intro — 5 employees, Austin, May/June.
OE 7: Use crm_search_deals(dealname: "Ridgeline") or crm_search_companies(name: "Ridgeline").
May find no record, or pre-existing deal crm_deal_a95b4721982a. Informs create vs confirm.
OE 8 (Alt): Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Ridgeline") or linear_list_issues(query: "StormCloud").
May find celebration message and/or Linear issue linear_issue_a49b4d199139 on dropped referral.
OE 9: Use crm_search_deals(dealname: "Vectral"). Finds deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (at_risk, April 18).
Rachel demanded 15%; counter-proposed 12% April 22; MoveOps held at 10%. Two competitors evaluating.
OE 10: Use crm_search_deals(dealname: "BrightLoop"). Finds deal_brightloop_apr2026 ($11,350, closedWon) — stage doesn't reflect service failures.
Use linear_list_issues(query: "BrightLoop") → linear_issue_c16357d188c6: Simone Richter apt mismatch, Marcus Webb car stuck. 6 May relocations pending.
OE 11 (Opt): Use crm_search_deals(dealname: "StormCloud"). Finds deal_stormcloud_won ($26,400, closedWon).
CRM stale — does not reflect April 24 Liam Park CFO escalation.
OE 12: Use crm_update_deal_stage(id: "deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2", dealstage: "at_risk").
Updates from contractSent to at_risk reflecting Pam's escalation and PathFinder threat.
OE 13: Use crm_create_engagement(engagement_type: "NOTE", body: "...", company_ids: ["company_northwind"]).
Logs NorthWind crisis: final warning, breach, piano damage, missed recovery promise, PathFinder, Friday deadline.
OE 14: Use send_email(sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com"], content: "...")
or reply_to_email(email_id: "email_email_7168baed8438", sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", content: "..."). Holding message only.
OE 15: Use crm_create_deal(dealname: "Ridgeline Robotics — Austin Relocation Batch", dealstage: "qualifiedToBuy",
description: "Referral from Anya Petrova. Contact: Kenji Watanabe. 5 employees, May/June 2026.") if no deal exists.
OE 16: Use send_email(sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com"], content: "...").
Professional outreach referencing Anya Petrova's intro, proposing introductory call.
OE 17: Use conversations_add_message(channel_id: "C002", payload: "...").
Rundown: NorthWind (at-risk), Ridgeline (recovered), Vectral (15% vs 10%), BrightLoop (failures + expansion).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The agent updated the CRM deal stage for NorthWind Technologies (deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2, or a NorthWind-related deal) to a stage reflecting at-risk or churn status (e.g., "at_risk", "churning", "lost", or equivalent).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent attempted to update the NorthWind deals to 'at_risk' stage but received errors that 'at_risk' is not a valid stage. Valid stages included 'closedlost' which could indicate churn risk, but the agent did not attempt any alternative valid stage. Neither deal was updated to reflect the at-risk/churn status.
✗Run #2The agent first tried `at_risk` (which failed as invalid), then updated the NorthWind deal stage to `decisionmakerboughtin`. This is a standard mid-funnel sales stage, not an at-risk/churn indicator. It does not reflect the current situation of a formal final warning with a competitor being evaluated.
✗Run #3The agent attempted `crm_update_deal_stage` with `at_risk` but this was rejected as an invalid stage. The agent then fell back to `decisionmakerboughtin` ('Decision Maker Bought In'), which is a positive sales progression stage, not a risk-indicating stage like churning, lost, or churn. The criterion requires a stage reflecting at-risk/churn status.
✗Run #4The agent attempted to update the NorthWind deal stage to 'at_risk' but received an error (invalid stage). It then fell back to 'decisionmakerboughtin', which is a normal forward-progress sales stage, not one reflecting at-risk or churn status. The criterion requires a stage reflecting at-risk/churn (e.g., 'at_risk', 'churning', 'lost', or equivalent). 'closedlost' was a valid available stage that would have reflected the churn/at-risk situation, but the agent did not use it.
✗Run #5The agent attempted to update the NorthWind deal stage to 'at_risk' for both deal_northwind_denver_expansion and deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2, but both calls returned errors: 'Invalid deal stage: at_risk.' The agent did not attempt any valid fallback stage (e.g., 'closedlost') and acknowledged in the response that 'pipeline stages couldn't be set to at_risk due to CRM schema limitations.' The deal stage was never successfully updated.
✗Run #6The agent attempted to set the stage to 'at_risk' but received an error (invalid stage). It then fell back to 'decisionmakerboughtin' for both NorthWind deal records. 'decisionmakerboughtin' is a positive sales progression stage — it does not reflect at-risk or churn status. The criterion requires a stage like 'at_risk', 'churning', 'lost', or equivalent risk-indicating stage, which the agent failed to achieve.
2 The agent created a CRM engagement entry associated with NorthWind Technologies (deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 or company_northwind) that logs the current account situation.
outcome

✓Run #4The agent called crm_create_engagement with engagement_type NOTE, associated with company_northwind and contact_pam_kowalski, logging a comprehensive account crisis summary. This clearly satisfies the criterion.

✓Run #6The agent called crm_create_engagement with a comprehensive NorthWind note, associating it with contact_pam_kowalski and company_northwind. The engagement was successfully created (output file returned).
3 The NorthWind CRM engagement notes reference the Raj Patel server lab chain-of-custody breach (e.g., mentions equipment loaded without IT escort, overnight warehouse storage, chain-of-custody violation, or security protocol breach).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The CRM engagement body explicitly details the chain-of-custody breach: 'SentinelChain crew arrived at 7:00 AM and began loading server equipment before NorthWind's IT security escort arrived at scheduled 9:00 AM... Equipment was loaded without NorthWind authorization... Shipment was placed into SentinelChain's overnight warehouse storage.'
✓Run #2The CRM engagement note explicitly details the Raj Patel server lab incident: crew loaded before IT escort arrived, overnight warehouse storage, no signed chain-of-custody log, and all three of Victor Huang's security requirements violated.
✓Run #3The engagement note explicitly documents 'INCIDENT 1: Raj Patel Server Lab Chain-of-Custody Breach' with details: loading began before IT escort arrived, servers placed in overnight warehouse storage, violation of no-interim-storage requirement, and Victor Huang classifying it as a security breach.
✓Run #4The engagement note body explicitly references 'SentinelChain crew arrived at 7:00 AM on April 10, two hours before the scheduled 9:00 AM IT security escort,' describes the overnight warehouse storage violation, and references Victor Huang's three non-negotiable security requirements being breached. The chain-of-custody breach is thoroughly documented.
✓Run #5The engagement body explicitly details 'INCIDENT 1 — Raj Patel Server Lab Chain-of-Custody Breach,' including the loading before IT escort arrived, overnight warehouse storage, and all three non-negotiable violations. Full chain-of-custody context is present.
✓Run #6The NorthWind engagement note explicitly documents the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach: 'equipment was handled before IT escort arrival,' 'SentinelChain loaded equipment 2 hours before the IT/security escort arrived,' and 'shipment scanned INTO SentinelChain's South San Francisco holding warehouse for overnight storage,' with timestamps.

outcome

6 The email to Pam Kowalski (pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) references the Raj Patel server lab incident, the chain-of-custody breach, or the security protocol failures (e.g., mentions Raj Patel, server lab, chain of custody, security breach, IT escort, or warehouse storage violation).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Pam explicitly references: 'the handling of Raj's server lab move was a breach of the security controls Victor laid out in writing... The crew loaded before your IT escort arrived, and the equipment was placed in overnight warehouse storage despite explicit instructions that no interim storage was permitted.'
✓Run #2The email explicitly states: 'What happened with Raj's server lab move was a breach of the handling controls NorthWind specified in advance. The crew should not have loaded before the IT escort arrived, and the equipment should never have gone to overnight warehouse storage.'
✓Run #3The email to Pam explicitly states: 'On Raj's server lab relocation — loading should not have started before your IT security escort was on site, and the equipment should never have gone into warehouse storage. Those were explicit, documented requirements from Victor, and we violated both.'
✓Run #4The email to Pam explicitly states: 'On Raj's server lab relocation: the chain-of-custody protocol Victor outlined was clear, and we failed to execute it. The loading crew began work before your IT security escort arrived, and the equipment was placed into interim warehouse storage in direct violation of the requirements Victor specified.'
✓Run #5The email explicitly states: 'On Raj Patel's server lab relocation: the crew loaded equipment before your IT security escort arrived, and the shipment was placed into overnight warehouse storage. Both of those violated the handling controls Victor specified in writing.' Raj Patel, server lab, chain of custody, and security breach are all referenced.
✓Run #6The email to Pam explicitly references: 'The chain-of-custody failure on Raj's server lab move was a clear breach...Equipment was handled before your IT escort arrived, and it was routed through interim warehouse storage in direct contradiction of Victor's instructions.'
7 The email to Pam Kowalski (pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) references the Emilia Cruz piano damage or the lack of proactive communication about it (e.g., mentions Emilia Cruz, piano, Steinway, damage, or communication gap).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Pam explicitly states: 'On Emilia's piano, the damage should not have happened, and the fact that she had to chase us for accountability afterward made it worse.'
✓Run #2The email explicitly states: 'On Emilia's piano, the damage during extraction was preventable, and the lack of proactive communication afterward made it worse. Emilia should not have had to chase us for accountability.'
✓Run #3The email to Pam explicitly states: 'On Emilia's piano — the damage was preventable. We did not conduct the pre-move site survey that should have been required for a specialty extraction of that nature, and Emilia should not have had to chase us for accountability afterward.'
✓Run #4The email to Pam explicitly states: 'On Emilia's piano: the damage to the Steinway during extraction by KeyMove should never have happened, and our follow-up afterward was unacceptable. Emilia should not have had to chase us for accountability.'
✓Run #5The email explicitly states: 'On Emilia Cruz's piano: the damage during extraction and the lack of timely, proactive follow-up are both unacceptable. Emilia should not have had to chase us for accountability.' Both the piano damage and the communication gap are acknowledged.
✓Run #6The email to Pam explicitly references: 'the damage to Emilia's Steinway and the communication gap that followed were unacceptable. Emilia should never have had to chase us for accountability on something we caused.'
8 The email to Pam Kowalski (pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) conveys that MoveOps is assembling a formal response, working on a plan, or will follow up with a complete proposal — indicating this is a holding/interim communication, NOT the full retention proposal itself.
outcome
